meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome version control issues. A beginner-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop losing hours of progress by manually copying files. The Antigravity project template integrates automated versioning directly into your workspace. By pairing this with Claude Code, you gain an AI-native development environment that tracks every iteration, ensuring your code remains secure, recoverable, and ready for your professional portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a screen filled with folders named 'project_final_v2', 'project_final_final', and 'project_broken'. The anxiety of accidentally deleting a feature you spent all night building is real, and the lack of a proper version control strategy is the silent killer of every student's first SaaS project.

But the era of manual file management is over. By deploying the Antigravity project template, you transform your local environment into a professional-grade sandbox. We’re moving beyond simple backups into intelligent state management, letting you focus on the architecture while the tools handle the history.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffolding
steps.description: Standardize your directory structure to ensure all future versioning tools can hook into your project correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold with standard structure and enable automated git-based versioning for a beginner SaaS app.

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Context
steps.description: Grant Claude Code permission to scan your project files to provide intelligent refactoring suggestions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current directory and create a .claude-ignore file to exclude build artifacts and focus on source code.

steps.title: Implement Atomic Commit Strategy
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate commit messages based on logical feature completion rather than time-based saves.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my recent changes and write a professional, concise commit message following conventional commit standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'feat: implement user authentication flow'

steps.title: Establish Recovery Checkpoints
steps.description: Create 'Golden States' before making major architectural changes to ensure you can revert in seconds.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a new branch and tag it as 'stable-alpha' before I begin implementing the database integration.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout -b feature/db-integration && git tag stable-alpha

steps.title: Validate Integrity with AI
steps.description: Verify that your version history is clean and your project dependencies are correctly locked.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the project history and check for any common configuration drift that might break the build.

pro_tips: Always commit before calling an AI to refactor; it makes reverting a one-line command if the logic hallucinates.
Use descriptive branch names like 'fix/login-bug' rather than 'test1' to maintain a professional Git log for future recruiters.
Keep your .gitignore file updated; never commit your .env files or API keys to any repository.
Treat your commit messages as a documentation trail; explain 'why' you changed the code, not just 'what' you changed.
Sync your local repo to GitHub early; a remote backup is the only true insurance against hardware failure.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your version control is automated, start deploying your app to a free tier hosting service like Vercel or Netlify to see your project live on the web.
content.conversion_block: You now have the foundation to build without fear of data loss. Every great developer started exactly where you are today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
